cells.title: The Overflowing Moon Tank
cells.question: The tank is 3/4 full. A helper adds 1/2 tank. The tank holds 1 full tank. How much overflows?
hints: Draw the tank divided into fourths.
How many fourths equal one half?
Add the two amounts — does it exceed 1 full tank?
cells.world_feedback: The tank glows too bright and spills exactly 1/4 moon-fuel onto the workshop floor.
cells.transfer_question: If the tank is 2/3 full and you add 1/2, what happens?
cells.explanation: When two partial amounts combine, compare their sum to one whole. Here the total passes a full tank, so the extra spills out.

cells.question: The tank is 3/4 full. A helper adds 1/2 tank. The tank holds 1 full tank. How much overflows?
hints: Draw the tank divided into fourths.
How many fourths equal one half?
Add the two amounts — does it exceed 1 full tank?
cells.world_feedback: The tank glows too bright and spills exactly 1/4 moon-fuel onto the workshop floor.
cells.transfer_question: If the tank is 2/3 full and you add 1/2, what happens?
cells.explanation: When two partial amounts combine, compare their sum to one whole. Here the total passes a full tank, so the extra spills out.
